title: NOC 92011 – Supervisors, petroleum, gas and chemical processing and utilities | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors under NOC 92011 are the operational leaders of Canada's energy and chemical hubs. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals oversee the complex processes of refining crude oil, processing natural gas, manufacturing chemical products, and managing public utilities like water and power. In 2026, as Canada integrates "Blue Hydrogen" production and massive carbon-capture systems into its energy grid, these supervisors are at the absolute forefront of the global energy transition. For immigration, this role is a high-priority candidate for the Alberta Opportunity Stream and the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Level2Major Group92 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsSub-Major Group920 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsMinor Group9201 – Supervisors, processing and manufacturing occupationsNOC Code92011

duties: Supervise, coordinate, and schedule the activities of workers who operate petroleum refineries, chemical plants, and utility systems.
Establish work schedules and procedures and coordinate activities with maintenance and engineering departments to minimize downtime.
Ensure strict adherence to safety and environmental regulations, including the management of hazardous materials (WHMIS).
Resolve operational problems and recommend measures to improve productivity and resource recovery.
Train workers in job duties, advanced safety procedures (e.g., H2S Alive), and the operation of new digital control systems.
Requisition materials and supplies and manage equipment maintenance schedules.
Prepare detailed production, safety, and regulatory compliance reports for senior management.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Work Experience: Several years of experience in the occupations supervised (e.g., as a process operator or utility worker) are mandatory.
Education: A college diploma or university degree in chemical technology, power engineering, or a related field is increasingly preferred for modern automated plants.

Certifications: * A provincial Power Engineering Certificate (Class 4, 3, 2, or 1) is often required for energy and utility roles.Specialized safety certificates (H2S Alive, First Aid, WHMIS) are standard.
Specialized safety certificates (H2S Alive, First Aid, WHMIS) are standard.
Strong leadership, analytical thinking, and proficiency in Distributed Control Systems (DCS) are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, experienced supervisors in Alberta’s oil sands or offshore platforms in Newfoundland often earn annual salaries exceeding $145,000, inclusive of shift premiums.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate a steady increase in "Clean Energy" supervisory roles as Canada optimizes its traditional reservoirs while building new hydrogen and ammonia processing infrastructure. The "Hiring Gap Intensity" reported by industry experts suggests that qualified supervisors in the energy and utility sectors will have their choice of high-paying opportunities across Canada through the end of the decade.
labour_market_demand: The demand for petroleum and chemical processing supervisors is Very Good, particularly in Western Canada and the Atlantic offshore regions. In 2026, Alberta (Edmonton/Fort McMurray), Saskatchewan, and Newfoundland & Labrador are reporting significant "Hiring Difficulty" indices due to the expansion of natural gas exports and lithium extraction from oilfield brines. Furthermore, the utilities sector (water and power) is seeing a massive wave of retirements, creating a stable, long-term need for supervisory talent in Ontario and British Columbia.
Energy Transition Leadership: Lead the implementation of "Net-Zero" production technologies and carbon sequestration systems.
Premium Compensation: This is one of the highest-paying supervisory roles in Canada, often including remote work premiums and high-performance bonuses.
Global Innovation: Manage the world's most advanced chemical processing and utility monitoring software (SCADA/DCS).
Strategic PR Pathways: This occupation is a high priority for Express Entry Category-Based Draws for "Trade Occupations" and the Alberta Energy Stream.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Targeted for candidates with experience in "Trade Occupations."
Alberta Opportunity Stream: A high-priority pathway for supervisors currently working in Alberta’s energy or utility sectors.
Saskatchewan INP (Occupations In-Demand): Frequently prioritizes supervisors in the oil, gas, and chemical sectors.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): A reliable route for supervisors with the required work experience and a job offer.
